title: Implement Remaining 18 Avatar Trait Subclasses
description: The base class architecture is complete. Each remaining ADHD trait needs a concrete subclass implementing get_adhd_trait_impact() and simulate_struggle(). See the implementation_pattern section below for a complete code example. Reference implementation available in src/avatars/adhd_traits/attention_deficit.py.
pattern_to_follow: See implementation_pattern below for a complete example
required_methods: get_adhd_trait_impact(task_context) -> Dict with difficulty_modifier, quality_modifier, time_modifier, cognitive_load_modifier

implementation_pattern.description: Example implementation pattern for ADHD trait subclasses
implementation_pattern.example_code:
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from ..base_avatar import BaseAvatar

class ExampleTrait(BaseAvatar):
    """Avatar with [specific ADHD trait]."""
    
    def __init__(self, avatar_id: str, trait_config: Dict[str, Any], event_bus=None):
        super().__init__(avatar_id, trait_config, event_bus=event_bus)
        # Initialize trait-specific state from trait_config
        self.trait_intensity = trait_config.get('intensity', 0.7)
    
    def get_adhd_trait_impact(self, task_context: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Calculate trait impact on task performance."""
        # Analyze task context and trait state
        task_type = task_context.get('task_type', 'unknown')
        
        # Calculate modifiers based on trait
        difficulty_modifier = 1.0  # 1.0 = baseline, >1.0 = harder
        quality_modifier = 0.0     # 0.0 = no impact, <0 = quality reduction
        time_modifier = 1.0        # 1.0 = baseline, >1.0 = takes longer
        cognitive_load = 0.0       # 0.0-1.0 scale
        
        return {
            'difficulty_modifier': difficulty_modifier,
            'quality_modifier': quality_modifier,
            'time_modifier': time_modifier,
            'cognitive_load_modifier': cognitive_load,
            'struggle_indicators': self._generate_struggle_indicators(task_context)
        }
    
    def simulate_struggle(self, task_context: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[str]:
        """Simulate authentic trait-specific struggles."""
        struggles = []
        # Add struggle indicators based on task context and trait state
        if self._should_struggle_with_x(task_context):
            struggles.append('struggle_type_x')
        return struggles
    
    def _generate_struggle_indicators(self, task_context: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[str]:
        """Generate specific struggle indicators."""
        indicators = []
        # Implement trait-specific logic
        return indicators
    
    def _should_struggle_with_x(self, task_context: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """Determine if specific struggle should occur."""
        # Implement trait-specific logic
        return False
key_points: Inherit from BaseAvatar and call super().__init__() with event_bus parameter
Store trait-specific configuration from trait_config parameter
Implement get_adhd_trait_impact() returning all required modifiers
Implement simulate_struggle() returning list of struggle indicators
Use helper methods to organize trait-specific logic
Base calculations on task_context (task type, duration, complexity, etc.)
Make struggles authentic to the specific ADHD trait based on research

notes: Each trait should have unique struggle patterns based on ADHD research
Use the ExperienceMemory system (inherited from BaseAvatar) — don't bypass it
New Avatar constructor should accept optional EventBus for shared event bus
See implementation_pattern above for complete example code
